rationale: Both titles are well-structured and reference the same PRO-PVI study with suture-mediated femoral venous closure after PVI. Title X is concise and clear but provides only the intervention and outcome direction without quantification. Title Y adds the specific success rate (96%) and a concrete comparative metric (halving time to discharge), which substantially increases informativeness — a criterion Europace values for prospective studies reporting primary outcomes. The slight reduction in clarity for Y is acceptable given the added specificity; the sentence remains grammatically clean and the key numbers are immediately accessible to readers scanning abstracts.
loser_weakness: Title X omits quantitative results, leaving the reader without the study's primary finding. For a prospective trial reporting a specific success rate and discharge-time comparison, this represents a missed opportunity to convey the magnitude and clinical relevance of the results, making the title feel more like a study description than a results summary.
